metadata.title: Export Financing Product | Meezan Bank
metadata.description: The State Bank of Pakistan is committed to supporting exporters by ensuring uninterrupted access to credit. Aligned with this vision, Meezan Bank offers a comprehensive range of Export Financing solutions designed to meet the diverse needs of our valued customers.

content: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Export Financing Solutions The State Bank of Pakistan is committed to supporting exporters by ensuring uninterrupted access to credit. Aligned with this vision, Meezan Bank offers a comprehensive range of Export Financing solutions designed to meet the diverse needs of our valued customers. The Bank’s Export Finance Schemes provide concessional, short-term financing to exporters of manufacturing goods and services — with a special focus on value-added products. Key Features Pre-shipment Financing Murabaha Musawammah Istisna Tijarah Salam Running Musharakah Post-shipment Financing Murabaha / Musawammah Deferred Tijarah Running Musharakah Alternative to Export Bill Discounting Bai Salam Tijarah Running Musharakah – Alternate Export Bill Discounting (EBD) Non-Funded Facilities for Exporters by Meezan Bank Hedging Facilities (Wa’ad) Murabaha Meezan Bank offers Murabaha Financing, a sale-based transaction, wherein the Bank procures goods from the market and resells them to the exporter on a deferred payment basis, with full disclosure of both the cost price and the agreed profit margin, to meet the exporter’s raw material procurement needs. Musawammah Financing is also a sale-based transaction, in which procures goods from market and sells a deferred payment basis at a mutually negotiated price, without any reference to the Bank’s cost price, material procurement needs. Istisna is a sale and agency-based mode of financing in which Meezan Bank finances the manufacturing of specific goods based against an export order or contract, thereby fulfilling the exporter’s working capital requirements. The price and detailed specifications of the commodity are mutually determined and agreed upon by both parties. Tijarah Financing sale and agency-based facility wherein exporters sell their finished goods to the Bank on a cash basis. This arrangement enables exporters to meet their working capital needs by converting credit sales into immediate cash, thereby providing Shariah-compliant liquidity support. Salam sale and agency-based facility, wherein the Bank pays the full purchase price in advance for specified homogeneous goods, and the exporter undertakes to deliver the goods at a mutually agreed future date. This arrangement provides the exporter with advance funds against deferred delivery. Running Musharakah Under Running Musharakah financing, Meezan Bank enters into a partnership with the exporter by contributing capital and sharing profits according to a pre-agreed ratio, while losses are borne strictly in proportion to the respective capital contributions. The Bank holds the export bill as collateral, and the proceeds thereof may be retained by the Bank as an advance share of profit under the Musharakah, subject to final adjustment against the business's financial statements according to the agreed profit-sharing ratio. Murabaha / Musawammah Meezan Bank offers Murabaha/Musawammah financing to exporters only against a genuine purchase requirement, by accepting export bills as collateral; financing is provided for the procurement of raw materials under the Murabaha/Musawammah structure. Deferred Tijarah Meezan Bank purchases finished goods inventory from deferred payment basis, while appointing the exporter as its agent to sell these goods to the ultimate buyer (importer) and collect the sale proceeds. Following the successful export, the exporter submits export bill as collateral. The Bank receives the proceeds from the export bill, thereby completing the transaction. Running Musharakah partnership with the exporter, and sharing profits as per holds the export bills as collateral, and profit distribution is done as per agreed profit-sharing ratio. Bai Salam Meezan export bill as collateral and on a Salam basis purchases FCY against Pak rupees from the customer at the prevailing spot rate, to cater to the financing requirements of the customer. financing to exporters strictly export bills as collateral and extending financing the procurement of goods. The financing will be settled from the export proceeds of the bill. purchases finished goods deferred payment basis and appoints agent to sell the sale proceeds. Upon receives the proceeds thereof, transaction. Bill Discounting (EBD) enters into a Musharakah losses are borne in proportion to and from the export bill are the Bank as part of its share of profit, which is later adjusted based on business's financial statements and agreed profit-sharing ratio. Hedging Facilities (Wa’ad) To hedge against foreign currency price fluctuations, Bank offers a Shariah-compliant hedging facility on the basis of Wa’ad, in which the customer (exporter) promises to sell the foreign currency (FCY) to the Bank against PKR upon the receipt of export proceeds in the future. Refinance Schemes by Bank of Pakistan Meezan Bank is offering refinance facilities under the schemes offered Bank of Pakistan to facilitate economic development of the country. Available modes of financing: Islamic Export Refinance Scheme (IERS) Rupee-based discounting facility under Refinance Scheme (IERS) Islamic Long Term Finance Facility (ILTFF) Islamic Finance Facility for Renewable Energy (IFRE) Refinance Scheme (IERS) Meezan Bank offers refinance facilities to meet the short-term financing needs of its customers. Designed to address the unique requirements of exporters, this facility is available in both Part-I and Part-II of the State Bank of Pakistan’s Export Refinance Scheme. refinance facilities to provide financing against export bills and export receivables. This product is tailored diverse needs of exporters and is available for both post-shipment & pre-shipment export receivables as per the Enhanced Scope Export Refinance Scheme. Finance Facility (ILTFF) In order to facilitate export growth, Meezan Bank offers the Islamic Long-Term Financing Facility under Bank of Pakistan’s long term facility at concessionary rates for export-oriented units. This facility provides local currency financing for the purchase of new plant and machinery, whether imported pr locally manufactured, and is structured under the Diminishing Musharakah mode of financing. Renewable Energy (IFRE) Meezan Bank, in line with Bank of Pakistan’s initiative, offers the long-term Islamic Financing Renewable Energy (IFRE) to support the energy sector of the country via new and alternate energy projects to fulfill both the individual and industrial energy needs. Financing under IFRE shall be available for power projects using alternative/renewable energy sources (solar, wind, hydro, bio-fuels, bagasse cogeneration and geothermal as fuel) under mode of financing. EXIM-Administered Islamic Long Term Financing Facility for Plant and Machinery (E-ILTFF) Purpose Learn More Eligibility Learn More Financing Limit Learn More Tenor Learn More Pricing Learn More Purpose Long-term currency financing for imported and locally manufactured new plant and machinery. Eligibility Available to export-oriented projects with annual exports of at least USD 5 million or 50% of sales (whichever is lower). Eligible sectors under the scheme: Please note that as per IH&SMEFD Circular Letter No. 2 of 2020, dated January 28, 2020, the scope of ILTFF has been extended to all sectors permitted under the Export Policy Order issued by the Ministry of Commerce from time to time. Accordingly, the earlier instructions regarding sectoral eligibility criteria specified in IH&SMEFD Circular No. 1 of 2018, dated February 14, 2018, stand revised/withdrawn Financing Limit Maximum limit for a single export-oriented unit is Rs. 5 billion. Tenor Maximum 10 years including a maximum grace period of 2 years. Where is provided for a period of up to 5 years, the grace period shall not be more than one year. Pricing Applied Rate = SBP Policy Rate − 3% Islamic Export Finance Scheme (E-IEFS) Eligibility Learn More Purpose Short-term local currency financing to support working capital needs related to export-oriented production, raw materials, and operational expenses. Eligibility Direct/indirect exporters including manufacturers, trading companies, and service providers. Export contract/LC, finance application, demand promissory note, and undertaking (Part I). Twice annual export performance of eligible commodities (Part II). Valid Consolidated Export Overdue Certificate (as per E-IEFS scheme requirement), confirming that the exporter’s overdue export bills are less than 5% of the total exports of the preceding year. (Part I /II/ RBD) Tenor Up to 180 days. Rate − 3% MeezanGPT Assalam o Alaikum! How can I be of service to you today?
